content: The relative performance gaps between intrinsic curiosity modules (ICM), random network distillation (RND), and general curious agents can be explained by how each framework leverages unlabeled stored memories in the active learning SOTA 2024-2025 agent memory stack: curious agents that explicitly gate access to episodic memories of under-explored states outperform ICM and RND on long-horizon tasks because they re-use uncertain, unlabeled memories to generate new intrinsic reward signals instead of recalculating prediction error from scratch for every new state. This leads to a new hybrid framework where active learning memory pruning rules are used to selectively retain only high-uncertainty under-explored state memories to boost curious agent sample efficiency.
source_topics: curiosity driven reinforcement learning ICM RND compared to curious agent
trigger_topic: curiosity driven reinforcement learning ICM RND compared to curious agent
